name: Dominic Rapini
subject: Singing the Act Blues - It started in CT
body:
Chris Murphy in Meltdown

| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/7adb0e5710c6/when-we-vote-we-win-14762597?e=8763c56857)
---
|
---
| | | | |
---
| | | | | “Money and corruption are ruining the land, crooked politicians betray the working man, pocketing the profits and treating us like sheep, and we’re tired of hearing promises that we know they’ll never keep.” \- George Bernard Shaw
---
|
---
|
**ActBlues: Singing the Money-Laundering Blues**

---
| | | | | | | | | | [](https://dominicforct.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=ebcefd906ec01db66e79c886c&id=95e8102e2b&e=8763c56857)
---
| _**By Dominic Rapini, American Businessman and Political Commentator**_
**How a Once-Mighty Fundraising Platform Landed in Hot Water—and How I Helped Turn Up the Heat**
It’s official: ActBlue is having a worse year than the Chicago Bears. Once the crown jewel of Democratic fundraising, this progressive money machine is now drowning in allegations of money laundering, fraudulent donations, foreign interference, and general incompetence. If you listen closely, you can almost hear the sad trombone of campaign finance lawyers preparing their defense arguments.
**From Blue Wave to Red Flags**
For years, ActBlue has been the Democratic Party’s golden goose, funneling billions into campaigns while masquerading as a grassroots fundraising powerhouse. The pitch was simple: “Hey, just chip in $3 to save democracy!” And chip in people did—except, it turns out, some of those ‘people’ didn’t even know they were donating.
_That’s where I come in._
As a former Republican candidate in Connecticut and a cybersecurity professional, I started noticing unusual donation patterns tied to ActBlue back in 2018. What kind of patterns, you ask? Oh, just 86-year-old retirees allegedly donating thousands of times, often at oddly precise intervals, as if their credit cards had been hooked up to a donation IV drip.
One Yale professor—sorry, Yalze **(Yale was misspelled in FEC filings! )** professor, according to FEC filings—was recorded as making 2,132 donations totaling $75,000. Another elderly gentleman supposedly made 12,892 donations over 228 months—which, if true, suggests this guy was donating from beyond the grave, or close to it.
And it wasn’t just Democrats who were getting in on the action. A 72-year-old registered Republican from Madison, CT somehow managed to contribute $101,000 in a single year, 29 times more than in previous elections. Funny thing—nobody in Connecticut Republican circles had ever heard of her. Yes, WinRed was occasionally fishy too.
So I did what any reasonable person would do: I knocked on her door. Three different visits. No answer each time. Suspicious? You bet. In fact, I successfully knocked on 5 doors and engaged the would be donors.**Each one (5) signed an affidavit attesting to the fact that donations I showed them were NO THEIR OWN!**
**Follow the (Possibly Laundered) Money**
When we dug deeper, we found that ActBlue’s recurring small-dollar donations weren’t just unusual—they were perfectly engineered to skirt campaign finance laws.
Here’s how the scheme may have worked:1️⃣ Mystery money enters the system—potentially from unaudited federal funds, foreign sources, or donors who didn’t know they were donating.
2️⃣ ActBlue breaks it up into thousands of micropayments under fake or unwitting donors’ names, keeping each transaction under $200 to avoid FEC reporting requirements.
3️⃣ **ALLEGEDLY** : The laundered money gets redirected into Democratic campaigns and activist groups, appearing as small, grassroots contributions.
4️⃣ When exposed, Democratic leadership pretends to know nothing.Sound crazy? Maybe. But when $1.88 million is funneled through just 18 elderly donors in Connecticut alone, it raises a few mild concerns.
**ActBlue’s Response: Running for the Exits**
If ActBlue had nothing to hide, you’d think they’d be eager to clear their name. Instead, they’re acting like a Wall Street exec after the SEC shows up with subpoenas.
**Since February 2025, at least seven senior officials have fled the company faster** than a leprechaun escaping a tax audit. Meanwhile, ActBlue’s remaining legal counsel is **accusing leadership** of retaliation, labor unions are calling for independent investigations, and donors are starting to ask where, exactly, their money has been going.
The panic is so real that ActBlue has even canceled meetings with political strategists—the equivalent of a restaurant shutting down reservations right before the dinner rush.
And let’s not forget **Democratic Senator Chris Murphy,** who has been eerily silent about the allegations. Funny, considering he’s been the loudest advocate for small-dollar donations—and his own FEC filings show donation patterns that look a little too perfect. (Murphy, buddy, people don’t donate four times a month, twice in the same day, unless they’re trying to meet their Starbucks Rewards quota.) **See Graphic Below:**
**What Happens Next?** As the walls close in, Republicans are pressing for investigations at the federal and state levels. There’s talk of subpoenas, forensic audits, and even FBI involvement. If the allegations stick, ActBlue could be looking at serious legal consequences, massive fines, and—best case scenario—a complete overhaul of how online campaign fundraising works.
For the Democrats, this couldn’t come at a worse time. If ActBlue goes down, they lose their most powerful fundraising tool, leaving them scrambling to find a replacement cash pipeline before 2026.
**Final Thoughts: The ActBlues Are Real** Look, I don’t take joy in seeing an entire political fundraising machine crash and burn. (Okay, maybe just a little.) But what I do take joy in is transparency, accountability, and exposing what looks like one of the biggest campaign finance scandals in recent history.
So the next time a politician asks for your **“small-dollar donation to protect democracy,”** maybe ask who’s really behind that transaction—and whether it’s coming from your credit card, some unsuspecting retiree, or a mystery bank account in Venezuela.
ActBlue is singing the blues—and I’m proud to have played a part in turning up the volume.
🚨 If you want to see the full research and data behind these findings, stay tuned. This story is just getting started. 🚨

Funny thing—nobody in Connecticut Republican circles had ever heard of her. Yes, WinRed was occasionally fishy too. So I did what any reasonable person would do: I knocked on her door. Three different visits. No answer each time. Suspicious? You bet. In fact, I successfully knocked on 5 doors and engaged the would be donors.Each one (5) signed an affidavit attesting to the fact that donations I showed them were NO THEIR OWN! Follow the (Possibly Laundered) Money When we dug deeper, we found that ActBlue’s recurring small-dollar donations weren’t just unusual—they were perfectly engineered to skirt campaign finance laws. Here’s how the scheme may have worked:1️⃣ Mystery money enters the system—potentially from unaudited federal funds, foreign sources, or donors who didn’t know they were donating. 2️⃣ ActBlue breaks it up into thousands of micropayments under fake or unwitting donors’ names, keeping each transaction under $200 to avoid FEC reporting requirements. 3️⃣ ALLEGEDLY : The laundered money gets redirected into Democratic campaigns and activist groups, appearing as small, grassroots contributions. 4️⃣ When exposed, Democratic leadership pretends to know nothing.Sound crazy? Maybe. But when $1.88 million is funneled through just 18 elderly donors in Connecticut alone, it raises a few mild concerns. ActBlue’s Response: Running for the Exits If ActBlue had nothing to hide, you’d think they’d be eager to clear their name. Instead, they’re acting like a Wall Street exec after the SEC shows up with subpoenas. Since February 2025, at least seven senior officials have fled the company faster than a leprechaun escaping a tax audit. Meanwhile, ActBlue’s remaining legal counsel is accusing leadership of retaliation, labor unions are calling for independent investigations, and donors are starting to ask where, exactly, their money has been going. The panic is so real that ActBlue has even canceled meetings with political strategists—the equivalent of a restaurant shutting down reservations right before the dinner rush. And let’s not forget Democratic Senator Chris Murphy, who has been eerily silent about the allegations. Funny, considering he’s been the loudest advocate for small-dollar donations—and his own FEC filings show donation patterns that look a little too perfect. (Murphy, buddy, people don’t donate four times a month, twice in the same day, unless they’re trying to meet their Starbucks Rewards quota.) See Graphic Below: What Happens Next? As the walls close in, Republicans are pressing for investigations at the federal and state levels. There’s talk of subpoenas, forensic audits, and even FBI involvement. If the allegations stick, ActBlue could be looking at serious legal consequences, massive fines, and—best case scenario—a complete overhaul of how online campaign fundraising works. For the Democrats, this couldn’t come at a worse time. If ActBlue goes down, they lose their most powerful fundraising tool, leaving them scrambling to find a replacement cash pipeline before 2026. Final Thoughts: The ActBlues Are Real Look, I don’t take joy in seeing an entire political fundraising machine crash and burn. (Okay, maybe just a little.) But what I do take joy in is transparency, accountability, and exposing what looks like one of the biggest campaign finance scandals in recent history. So the next time a politician asks for your “small-dollar donation to protect democracy,” maybe ask who’s really behind that transaction—and whether it’s coming from your credit card, some unsuspecting retiree, or a mystery bank account in Venezuela. ActBlue is singing the blues—and I’m proud to have played a part in turning up the volume. 🚨 If you want to see the full research and data behind these findings, stay tuned. name: History Facts
subject: A Roman emperor declared war on... the sea?!
body:
To say that Caligula was not well liked in his time is putting it lightly, as the first historians who wrote of him are said to have been so biased against the eccentric Roman emperor that it’s difficult to distinguish fact from fiction.
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=1krv9cfc68dhvqd6cxccce5oo40l4ul782243ymtda7ud6vtsn41lh7sl05jilhxf7liwvbnn9ms0odf13sxgy4f4v8ix3ffz9c0szgy2sxeql60qqp0w7xv1ewk5a9d8kc1v1lv9hpg0el93c8bh78f3&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=stbgfo&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
---
# [The Roman Emperor Caligula declared war on the sea.](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=stbgfo&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
### [World History](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=stbgfo&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=stbgfo&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
| T o say that Caligula was not well liked in his time is putting it lightly, as the first historians who wrote of him are said to have been so biased against the eccentric Roman emperor that it’s difficult to distinguish fact from fiction. Nevertheless, his erratic behavior is well documented, including the fact that he [once declared war on the sea](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=stbgfo&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com). He appears to have done so in order to declare a symbolic victory on the vast ocean. Roman historian Suetonius wrote of the incident in 40 CE, “And while no one could imagine what he intended to do, he suddenly commanded them to gather up seashells, and fill their helmets and the folds of their tunics with them, calling them ‘the spoils of the sea due to the Capitoline and the Palatine.’”
---
The seashells were then brought back to Rome, though historian David Woods has suggested that “seashells” is a mistranslation of the word “conchae,” which was also used to denote British ships captured in the English Channel. To celebrate his triumph, Caligula had a lighthouse built, gave his soldiers 100 denarii (Roman silver coins) each, and told them, “Go your ways and be merry; for now you are rich!”
[](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=stbgfo&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) | [](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=stbgfo&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com)
---
By clicking a link in this email, you're agreeing to our [Terms of Use](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=2kh5r9m0gvwoxxm79p4pr4j9otemdid3hs3qs7zzyg5k3vrjsfh12yxmgkkstwwzlr87y5p7r99oa34ypscbz5ixch2za436wlhc7tfmgb0gfxm6zt3vg2e5x9it4nwv8llt66s5f6sb3ekn8x6zxhj4nqhdbuwfod5voctmel8f0&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=stbgfo&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) and [Privacy Policy](https://track.historyfacts.com/?xtl=463ko1dghoubu15x7wzuir7zznecxne8li25xsozo0tt8udk70s2m8hf2uwzc47w5kk4aofry6m0baumwzhr4s2irzy0ew89w96bwc3648n2pfh3lxyy19ao0wd9siaxaz2x9rtdxelnvgjuaf99vg2j7t0g46rtkiud2hjbje59i193&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=stbgfo&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com).
---

| | | Did you know?
---|---|---
## A movie based on Caligula’s life was banned in several countries.
Any movie that properly conveyed the emperor’s life was bound to be controversial, but the filmmakers behind _Caligula_ probably didn’t expect the level of pushback they received. Produced by _Penthouse_ founder Bob Guccione, who wanted to make the film as explicit as possible, _Caligula_ was [disavowed](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=stbgfo&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com) by screenwriter Gore Vidal and original director Tinto Brass due to extensive changes made during production. Guccione refused to submit the film to the MPAA, which he assumed would grant it an “X” rating, and instead gave it a “Mature Audiences” label. Among the countries that went even further by banning it outright for its graphic sexual content were Russia, Belarus, Canada, and Iceland, while England cut more than eight minutes before deeming it acceptable or even legal to screen. The countries that did allow it still didn’t like it — _Caligula_ received overwhelmingly negative reviews, with Roger Ebert calling it “sickening, utterly worthless, shameful trash” in his [zero-star review](https://track.historyfacts.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=stbgfo&__onlt=h&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com). Contemporary appraisals have been kinder, especially with 2023’s release of the “Ultimate Cut,” which is 17 minutes longer than the theatrical version and intended to be closer to Vidal’s vision.
---

clean_body: To say that Caligula was not well liked in his time is putting it lightly, as the first historians who wrote of him are said to have been so biased against the eccentric Roman emperor that it’s difficult to distinguish fact from fiction. | T o say that Caligula was not well liked in his time is putting it lightly, as the first historians who wrote of him are said to have been so biased against the eccentric Roman emperor that it’s difficult to distinguish fact from fiction. Nevertheless, his erratic behavior is well documented, including the fact that he . He appears to have done so in order to declare a symbolic victory on the vast ocean. Roman historian Suetonius wrote of the incident in 40 CE, “And while no one could imagine what he intended to do, he suddenly commanded them to gather up seashells, and fill their helmets and the folds of their tunics with them, calling them ‘the spoils of the sea due to the Capitoline and the Palatine.’” The seashells were then brought back to Rome, though historian David Woods has suggested that “seashells” is a mistranslation of the word “conchae,” which was also used to denote British ships captured in the English Channel. To celebrate his triumph, Caligula had a lighthouse built, gave his soldiers 100 denarii (Roman silver coins) each, and told them, “Go your ways and be merry; for now you are rich!” | By clicking a link in this email, you're agreeing to our and . By the Numbers | | Length (in years) of Caligula’s reign 4 | | Length (in years) of Augustus’ reign, the longest of any Roman emperor 40 | Length (in years) of Augustus’ reign, the longest of any Roman emperor 40 | | Caligula’s age when he was assassinated in 41 CE 28 | | Total Roman emperors 70+ | Total Roman emperors 70+ | | | Did you know? ---|---|--- A movie based on Caligula’s life was banned in several countries. Any movie that properly conveyed the emperor’s life was bound to be controversial, but the filmmakers behind Caligula probably didn’t expect the level of pushback they received. Produced by Penthouse founder Bob Guccione, who wanted to make the film as explicit as possible, Caligula was by screenwriter Gore Vidal and original director Tinto Brass due to extensive changes made during production. Guccione refused to submit the film to the MPAA, which he assumed would grant it an “X” rating, and instead gave it a “Mature Audiences” label. Among the countries that went even further by banning it outright for its graphic sexual content were Russia, Belarus, Canada, and Iceland, while England cut more than eight minutes before deeming it acceptable or even legal to screen. The countries that did allow it still didn’t like it — Caligula received overwhelmingly negative reviews, with Roger Ebert calling it “sickening, utterly worthless, shameful trash” in his . Contemporary appraisals have been kinder, especially with 2023’s release of the “Ultimate Cut,” which is 17 minutes longer than the theatrical version and intended to be closer to Vidal’s vision. name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 3/18/2025
body:
Colorado’s 2025 legislature—a case study in big government overreach
| | | | [](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| | Hi friends,
Hope you had a lucky St. Patricks Day yesterday! 🍀

Here is a summary of today's top stories: ****
**🏛️ Colorado Legislative Session Updates...** Kevin Lundberg critiques Colorado’s legislature as an example of [unchecked government expansion](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), arguing that the current leadership is systematically eroding personal freedoms and imposing excessive regulations.
🏔️ **Here in Colorado...** UCCS faces a federal probe over [alleged race-based hiring](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) and scholarships as part of Trump's DEI crackdown. Energy producers warn [excessive regulations](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) are crippling investment and driving businesses out. Democrat Manny Rutinel has [raised over $1M](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) in his challenge against Gabe Evans, backed by national liberal groups. A top DEA official calls Colorado ["ground zero" for violent crime](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), citing cartel and gang activity.
🇺🇸**On the National Stage...** President Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin are expected to discuss a [proposed division of Ukrainian territory](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), including control over key power plants.

🗣️ **Commentary Spotlight...** A new column exposes the fearmongering and misinformation surrounding [school choice in Douglas County](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), where the fight for the John Adams Academy highlights the broader battle over educational freedom.****

This is the Mountain Minute for Tuesday, March 18, 2025. Don't forget to to listen to our new **Mountain Minute Podcast** , every morning! Click [here](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), or watch on [YouTube](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) or [Rumble](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).
– Heidi Ganahl
---
|
---
**TODAY'S TOP STORIES**
**[Lundberg: Colorado’s 2025 legislature—a case study in big government overreach](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** BY KEVIN LUNDBERG, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
The Colorado legislature is more than halfway through this year’s session.
Like the past six years of the Polis administration, this session has seen radical bills roar through the legislature with many passing on party-line votes.
If the governor is consistent with what he has done in the past he will sign virtually all of these extreme bills and continue to fundamentally transform Colorado.
This is a brief overview of bad, dumb and dangerous bills that are currently moving through the Colorado legislature. For a more complete listing of the worst bills I have identified, check out the weekly Lundberg Report I publish on my website, KevinLundberg.com.
**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
The Rocky Mountain Voice is a 501(c)(3) that exists to promote informed civic engagement and the principles of self-governance through enterprise reporting on public policy, politics, government, and culture throughout the state of Colorado. The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support!
[DONATE TO ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
|
---
**[Choked out: How Colorado’s regulatory maze is starving its energy producers](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Colorado's energy industry, particularly small and mid-sized operators, is struggling under a maze of regulations, delayed permits, and rising fines, making it harder to stay in business.
* The Energy & Carbon Management Commission (ECMC) has ballooned in scope, increasing fines and fees on energy producers, leading to concerns that enforcement is being used as a revenue source rather than for legitimate oversight.
* Experts in the energy sector argue that excessive regulations are driving companies out of Colorado, warning that the state will soon rely on imported energy while losing critical tax revenue from oil and gas production.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[UCCS under scrutiny for alleged race-based policies in higher education shakeup](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* UCCS is among over 50 universities facing scrutiny from the U.S. Department of Education as part of the Trump administration's effort to dismantle race-based Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) programs and enforce merit-based education policies.
* The investigation focuses on UCCS's College of Business, which allegedly used race-exclusionary practices in faculty recruitment through The PhD Project, a program that limits eligibility based on race. The administration argues such policies violate federal civil rights laws.
* Conservatives see the crackdown as a necessary correction to decades of leftist race-based policies, while left-wing activists and university officials claim it infringes on academic freedom. Legal challenges from major teachers' unions are already underway.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Ganahl: Smears and fearmongering—The fight for John Adams Academy and school choice in Douglas County](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* The fight to open John Adams Academy, a classical charter school in Douglas County, has been met with fierce opposition, including personal attacks, misinformation, and smear campaigns labeling it as extremist.
* Supporters argue that the academy would provide much-needed educational choice, focusing on a rigorous curriculum rooted in classical education principles, while opponents claim it threatens public schools and community dynamics.
* Despite the backlash, the school has gained strong backing from parents and community members, with over 200 families signing letters of intent, signaling a growing demand for alternatives to traditional public education.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
**[Colorado ‘ground zero for some of the most violent criminals in America’ says acting DEA administrator](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** [DENVER7](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Acting DEA Administrator Derek Maltz stated that Colorado has become “ground zero” for some of the most violent criminals in the U.S., with the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua establishing command and control in the state.
* Maltz credited the DEA’s Rocky Mountain Division for gathering intelligence on criminal networks, particularly Tren de Aragua, and emphasized their role in ongoing enforcement actions against organized crime.
* Maltz urged local leaders to set aside political differences and prioritize public safety, stressing that violent crime and cartel activity should be a bipartisan concern.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
|
---
** ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ **
**[Mesa County Republican Women’s Lincoln Day Dinner to Feature Congresswoman Harriet Hageman](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY RMV STAFF | LOCAL | MARCH 17** _The Mesa County Republican Women (MCRW) are set to host their highly anticipated Lincoln Day Dinner on Saturday, March 22, 2025, at the Clifton Community Center. This year’s keynote speaker is none other than Congresswoman Harriet Hageman, Wyoming’s sole representative in the U.S. House and a staunch advocate for conservative values and policies._ __
**[Three candidates, three strategies: The battle for CO GOP Chair](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY RMV STAFF | STATE | MARCH 16** _With the Colorado GOP Chair race heating up, more candidates have weighed in on the party’s future. When we first posed these questions, Brita Horn was the sole candidate to reply. Now, Darcy Schoening and Kevin McCarney have shared their perspectives, offering distinct strategies on voter outreach, fundraising and Proposition 108._
**[Who gets to know? Lawmakers, media and watchdogs wrangle over public records access](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**[](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)****BY RMV STAFF****| STATE | MARCH 13** _Last year Democrat lawmakers created a loophole to dodge public scrutiny—and Governor Polis made it law. Now it’s easier for lawmakers to evade state open meetings law, through written and electronic communication._
__

The only way we can continue to spread truth is with your support! | Colorado's energy industry, particularly small and mid-sized operators, is struggling under a maze of regulations, delayed permits, and rising fines, making it harder to stay in business. The Energy & Carbon Management Commission (ECMC) has ballooned in scope, increasing fines and fees on energy producers, leading to concerns that enforcement is being used as a revenue source rather than for legitimate oversight. Experts in the energy sector argue that excessive regulations are driving companies out of Colorado, warning that the state will soon rely on imported energy while losing critical tax revenue from oil and gas production. | UCCS is among over 50 universities facing scrutiny from the U.S. Department of Education as part of the Trump administration's effort to dismantle race-based Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) programs and enforce merit-based education policies. The investigation focuses on UCCS's College of Business, which allegedly used race-exclusionary practices in faculty recruitment through The PhD Project, a program that limits eligibility based on race. The administration argues such policies violate federal civil rights laws. Conservatives see the crackdown as a necessary correction to decades of leftist race-based policies, while left-wing activists and university officials claim it infringes on academic freedom. Legal challenges from major teachers' unions are already underway. | The fight to open John Adams Academy, a classical charter school in Douglas County, has been met with fierce opposition, including personal attacks, misinformation, and smear campaigns labeling it as extremist. Supporters argue that the academy would provide much-needed educational choice, focusing on a rigorous curriculum rooted in classical education principles, while opponents claim it threatens public schools and community dynamics. Despite the backlash, the school has gained strong backing from parents and community members, with over 200 families signing letters of intent, signaling a growing demand for alternatives to traditional public education. | Acting DEA Administrator Derek Maltz stated that Colorado has become “ground zero” for some of the most violent criminals in the U.S., with the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua establishing command and control in the state. Maltz credited the DEA’s Rocky Mountain Division for gathering intelligence on criminal networks, particularly Tren de Aragua, and emphasized their role in ongoing enforcement actions against organized crime. Maltz urged local leaders to set aside political differences and prioritize public safety, stressing that violent crime and cartel activity should be a bipartisan concern. | ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ RMV STAFF | LOCAL | MARCH 17 The Mesa County Republican Women (MCRW) are set to host their highly anticipated Lincoln Day Dinner on Saturday, March 22, 2025, at the Clifton Community Center. This year’s keynote speaker is none other than Congresswoman Harriet Hageman, Wyoming’s sole representative in the U.S. House and a staunch advocate for conservative values and policies. RMV STAFF | STATE | MARCH 16 With the Colorado GOP Chair race heating up, more candidates have weighed in on the party’s future. When we first posed these questions, Brita Horn was the sole candidate to reply. Now, Darcy Schoening and Kevin McCarney have shared their perspectives, offering distinct strategies on voter outreach, fundraising and Proposition 108. RMV STAFF| STATE | MARCH 13 Last year Democrat lawmakers created a loophole to dodge public scrutiny—and Governor Polis made it law. Now it’s easier for lawmakers to evade state open meetings law, through written and electronic communication. Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share? We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to mailto:tori@rockymountainvoice.com?subject=Contributor%20Interest%20\(via%20Mountain%20Minute\)) or send us a message on social media to chat! | | 🤔 DID YOU KNOW? Niagara Falls, one of the world's most famous waterfalls, became the nation's first state park in 1885. The park was designed by Frederick Law Olmsted, the renowned landscape architect behind New York City's Central Park. | CARTOON OF THE DAY | QUOTE OF THE DAY "The most wasted of days is one without laughter." — E.E. Cummings | | | | ---|---|--- Did a friend forward you this email? | A product of Rocky Mountain Voice, Inc. 8200 S Quebec St, STE A3601 Centennial, CO 80112 This message was originally sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com Be sure to put “RockyMountainVoice.com” on your safe-senders whitelist. If you ever stop receiving our emails, it might be because someone to whom you forwarded the email unintentionally removed you from the list. No worries; it is easy enough to reactivate your subscription immediately by visiting: Before you click the link below... If someone forwarded this email to you, please don’t! Clicking the link will end the subscription of dpwillis67@gmail.com []()

name: Broad + Liberty Staff
subject: App is a game changer for PA politics
body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/88a2f8a2c005/broad-liberty-and-politicspa-announce-new-strategic-partnership-for-5857489?e=b7b3eddce2)
---
| | | |
---
|
# **_Broad \+ Liberty_ and _PoliticsPA_ Mobile Apps are LIVE on [iOS](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=335921d474&e=b7b3eddce2) and [Android](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=9f5777234e&e=b7b3eddce2)**
---
| | | | If you’re reading this, you already know we’re the only place you can find certain stories in Pennsylvania—the ones other outlets won’t touch, the ones that dig deeper, and the straightforward political news that keeps you ahead of the spin. Now, we’re bringing that news straight to you—faster, clearer, and without the noise. The _[Broad + Liberty](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=9563868cc0&e=b7b3eddce2)_ and _[PoliticsPA](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=c95b22126a&e=b7b3eddce2)_ mobile apps are here, and they’re the fastest way to get Pennsylvania’s real news.

Here’s why you need these apps—right now:
* **News That Won’t Wait** – Real-time alerts for major political shifts, investigative bombshells, and key election updates. No more scrolling endlessly for the facts.
* **No Algorithm, No Censorship** – Get Pennsylvania’s biggest stories without algorithms deciding what’s “worthy” of your attention. No suppression, no shadowbans—just the news.
* **Exclusive Stories** – Some of our best work—insider reports, hard-hitting analysis, and political deep dives—will be available only on the app. If you wait for it to hit social media, you might miss it entirely.

We’re just getting started. More apps from our network are coming soon, bringing even more must-know journalism and political news straight to your pocket. Real news, real time, right here. Don’t wait for the news to find you. Go get it!
* Download the _Broad + Liberty_ app now → [iOS](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=0a9d0e8ecb&e=b7b3eddce2) or [Android](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=fd850ace6c&e=b7b3eddce2)
* Download the _PoliticsPA_ app now → [iOS](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=11c9cf6cf8&e=b7b3eddce2) or [Android](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=291fc18c34&e=b7b3eddce2)

---
|

| | | |
---

Broad + Liberty

Team,

_The end of the quarter is just around the corner, and it 's crunch time for my campaign. This first major deadline of 2025 is our fourth quarter, and we must finish strong._

**This deadline is like the final play of the game. It 's the witching hour - where good teams find a way to win, and bad teams come up short.** We're in the red zone, and [**we need your help to punch it in for a touchdown.**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=4826695e4b2f43eb98aa2660a80cfdf0&_e=YtxrsVUzJXY-n8E03AAp5nM0jC8NKUGgEomPLI87j4B60id1G-n6VKnyoqDe1FaQtXdZdmWlJowIPKprwWqpci0y_LS7q5QK0Xek7VX96O1PAP5pNXlzG7YupefVeUlQUwnjy4Hf1NtNgvx52GWoUB4RZvgbN3mbkPxnsLkViwBCSa3cwmqsV2LDWYClM4ihqVgq7Mdc8bgZs4c4qgGoTlVFuTimv7bmqBMma_pkELwfLFNEckxIwE71Q67cILz3TrJt_9r9ikdWj3tPsG2_VCgxmfa9hJZhfXbGdE5Qyu0zM2OMq_iPrXiwa2uxd_bzkqlFxE-JJq5BKLBHbLqSfw%3D%3D) The Radical Left is out there making their moves, pulling out all the stops, and throwing everything they've got to stop us.

[**🏈 HELP TEAM TUBERVILLE SCORE A TOUCHDOWN! 🏈**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=4826695e4b2f43eb98aa2660a80cfdf0&_e=YtxrsVUzJXY-n8E03AAp5nM0jC8NKUGgEomPLI87j4B60id1G-n6VKnyoqDe1FaQtXdZdmWlJowIPKprwWqpci0y_LS7q5QK0Xek7VX96O1PAP5pNXlzG7YupefVeUlQUwnjy4Hf1NtNgvx52GWoUB4RZvgbN3mbkPxnsLkViwBCSa3cwmqsV2LDWYClM4ihsLveBU2ZoqRzZBwEfKMCaXXJ98RNBRrmlp8e-eod-ftxCRmsbyoQwrpGBSNeyTEe0rJTVWFu7yT9c0HL0Yosci36NqB7t6ZaT66D96NRNRLB2apFyJwJDEIXSpA-mFAO29X-jg5p5b8hnawfs7keLQ%3D%3D)
_But just like in football, we can 't let up now._ We've been fighting hard, and now it's time to make sure we cross the goal line. We need to hit our fundraising goal by midnight, or we risk losing all the momentum we've built.

_We know what 's at stake here--__**America 's future.**_ The Radical Left is throwing everything at us, and we need your support to keep fighting. This isn't the time to fumble the ball, and it's certainly not the time to sit on the sidelines.

[**Can I count on you to help us make this final push and meet our end-of-quarter goal? A donation of $15, $20, or even $50, or whatever you can afford, will help us keep the ball moving down the field.**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=4826695e4b2f43eb98aa2660a80cfdf0&_e=YtxrsVUzJXY-n8E03AAp5nM0jC8NKUGgEomPLI87j4B60id1G-n6VKnyoqDe1FaQtXdZdmWlJowIPKprwWqpci0y_LS7q5QK0Xek7VX96O1PAP5pNXlzG7YupefVeUlQUwnjy4Hf1NtNgvx52GWoUB4RZvgbN3mbkPxnsLkViwBCSa3cwmqsV2LDWYClM4ihr_yzownWCUHkoz9CXH_i6fQavLeaIZGhp7wnrRjWj6tf3oeFPYhGCnCV7TGnH0mC9U6oa7ksOmNOSjkHsDkoDt06vqomABhywrT7g1wAKyOmfK25bthOcIv4QjH5gZrU3L9MJ4BbHa7MussZ2t9pbg%3D%3D)

[**🏈 DONATE NOW AND HELP US SECURE A VICTORY FOR AMERICA! 🏈**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=4826695e4b2f43eb98aa2660a80cfdf0&_e=YtxrsVUzJXY-n8E03AAp5nM0jC8NKUGgEomPLI87j4B60id1G-n6VKnyoqDe1FaQtXdZdmWlJowIPKprwWqpci0y_LS7q5QK0Xek7VX96O1PAP5pNXlzG7YupefVeUlQUwnjy4Hf1NtNgvx52GWoUB4RZvgbN3mbkPxnsLkViwBCSa3cwmqsV2LDWYClM4ihvgp8HmZPSNJOuald2S2GFTW4tYluiMrwhBZhE3Tb96a3YPVt8uFTFM4TI0cHLSPsegCsSGL5Y3xM-cOr8n7OYdBl6uE-jfFFWd5n_xBmjao1Zp-WUmSiF2Ux1BIkkUe9EMfoMRe21Cupm7W28G0-Gg%3D%3D)
Just like in football, it's all about teamwork. Together, we'll finish strong and keep fighting for the America-First Agenda. Thank you for your support.

God Bless,
_Coach Tuberville_

| [](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=4826695e4b2f43eb98aa2660a80cfdf0&_e=YtxrsVUzJXY-n8E03AAp5nM0jC8NKUGgEomPLI87j4B60id1G-n6VKnyoqDe1FaQtXdZdmWlJowIPKprwWqpci0y_LS7q5QK0Xek7VX96O1PAP5pNXlzG7YupefVeUlQUwnjy4Hf1NtNgvx52GWoUB4RZvgbN3mbkPxnsLkViwBCSa3cwmqsV2LDWYClM4ihaGqxK7HWN8nM1ozsLcOcYsF2xEMw9vRFFUNQnuM-ZLHkJSShuRWLiEbjyuDrHR4M7FriD4bzgqtJwtwsiJQgfp1Tw9NTDPDIZKnLwHwMzxjBJ0qzoqNJrRtbrVmDRR4M8IHO7MNw2wfspTNhDVIaeQ%3D%3D)

Meet Coach Tuberville:

**Coach Tuberville was born in Camden, Arkansas, the third of three children of Olive and Charles Tuberville. College Football 's 2004 Coach of the Year and two-time SEC Coach of the Year, Tommy Tuberville is most often recognized for his success on the field. But it is the legacy of his father, a highly decorated WWII veteran and Purple Heart recipient, that motivates Coach Tuberville to give back to the brave men and women of the United States Armed Forces.

In 1989 Coach Tuberville met his future wife, Suzanne Fette, at the Sugar Bowl in New Orleans. They were married in 1991 and have two boys, Tucker and Troy, who continue to make their dad proud.

In his career as a College Football Coach, Tommy started a long and successful career at the University of Miami, starting as a graduate assistant and rising to the Hurricanes' defensive coordinator. He led the Canes to three AP national championships and coached future Hall of Famers Ray Lewis and Warren Sapp, as well as Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson.

In 1998, Coach Tuberville was announced as the next head coach for the Auburn Tigers. Tuberville's time at Auburn was a success by any measure, as the Tigers captured eight consecutive bowl appearances, including five New Year's Day bowl appearances, five SEC Western Division titles, one SEC Championship, and a 13-0 season in 2004, after which Coach Tuberville was once again named the AP's SEC Coach of the Year. For his efforts during the perfect 2004 season, Tuberville would be named Paul "Bear" Bryant Coach of the Year, Associated Press Coach of the Year, Walter Camp Coach of the Year, Sporting News Coach of the Year, and AFCA Coach of the Year, among others.

_In 2020, Coach Tuberville became Senator Tuberville, winning his election to represent all Alabamians in the United States Senate with the full support of President Trump. Since then, he's supported President Trump's America-First policies while fighting off the Radical Left. In his first term as Senator, Tommy was one of eight Republicans to vote against certifying the 2020 election, has been a strong pro-life advocate, and called out Democrats for being pro-crime.

Now, Coach Tuberville is running for re-election so he can continue representing Alabamians and Americans in the United States Senate. The Radical Left has him circled as a prime target to defeat in the 2026 Midterm Elections. Can he count on your support? _

** [**HELP COACH TUBERVILLE**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=4826695e4b2f43eb98aa2660a80cfdf0&_e=YtxrsVUzJXY-n8E03AAp5nM0jC8NKUGgEomPLI87j4B60id1G-n6VKnyoqDe1FaQtXdZdmWlJowIPKprwWqpci0y_LS7q5QK0Xek7VX96O1PAP5pNXlzG7YupefVeUlQUwnjy4Hf1NtNgvx52GWoUB4RZvgbN3mbkPxnsLkViwBCSa3cwmqsV2LDWYClM4ihMu3c1ZwHJ6v0obo8WgCjHDdPPUFJT95lnSGsY4jDSwQ-gBI5FkQx4dk_055p7WXRiQGCTboAOFeZ2VFDQj1tB-FcAg4fLvZ7m544izGfKRlaePHZdYf80xkncl3UpwZ4hBwCVTKnqO7v_svDW1fz2g%3D%3D)

---

PO Box 590012, Birmingham, Alabama 35259

| Texans for Vaccine Choice was honored to join Mikki Willis, Matthew Guthrie, Steve Kirsch, and Brianne Dressen to **premiere the documentary “Follow the Silenced” at the Santa Monica Film Festival** this past weekend. The film tells the heartbreaking stories of Americans who volunteered for the first COVID vaccine trials in the US, and whose lives were upended from **devastating injuries resulting from the shots**. In painstaking detail, the filmmakers presented first-hand narratives, government data, patient files, and recordings all demonstrating that the public health experts tasked with ensuring vaccine safety were instead going to **great lengths to suppress the evidence of adverse events**. TFVC is proud to be credited as an Executive Producer for the movie, and delighted that the film was shown to a crowd of over five hundred viewers who cried and cheered throughout the event. And, **festival organizers awarded “Follow the Silenced” with “Best of Festival**!” This award belongs to the countless individuals who have worked tirelessly for decades to ensure the voices of the vaccine injured are heard. _Stay tuned... we can’t wait to bring this movie to you in the near future and for it to be released nationwide!_

name: Office of U.S. Senator Angela Alsobrooks
subject: Senator Alsobrooks Newsletter -- March 18
body:
|
---
Friends and neighbors,
I wanted to share with you why I voted 'No' on cloture and on the Republican continuing resolution, last week.

First things first, let me explain what happened.

House Republicans voted on a funding bill, a continuing resolution in this case.

Once it came to the Senate, each Member had the opportunity to decide whether we continue filibustering said resolution or not. I voted 'No' on cloture, which means I wanted to keep debating the bill. I felt we had both an opportunity and responsibility to ensure that any bill that passes our chamber didn't just offer a blank check to this Administration.

Why?

Because this continuing resolution would allow President Trump to continue doing what he's doing:

-Looting our government to pay for tax cuts for billionaires.

-Conducting a witch hunt against our patriotic civil servants -- people who work on behalf of our veterans, people who work on behalf of our students, people who work on behalf of our domestic violence survivors.

-Threatening to cut Social Security benefits, to cut Medicaid.

-Causing the markets to crash by starting a trade war with our allies that will raise prices on goods and impact the pocketbooks of everyday Americans.

To allow the President to continue what he's doing would be in direct contradiction with my mandate -- to fight for Marylanders.

We had a unique opportunity to course correct and pass a clean, bipartisan funding bill that would have addressed some of these concerns and mistakes.

That is why I voted 'No' last week.

This will not be the last fight we have. And I want you to know that I will always put Maryland, and the needs of Marylanders, first.

**Tariff Transparency Act**

I am proud to share that, last week, I introduced my first bill - the Tariff Transparency Act. This necessary legislation will require the United States International Trade Commission to conduct an investigation on the impact of these tariffs, and the threat of these tariffs, on imports from Mexico and Canada.

Marylanders are struggling to put food on the table and afford the basic necessities as it is. The last thing we need are tariffs that will raise prices even more. The truth is this: the Trump Administration knows that placing tariffs on allies like Canada and Mexico will raise prices for consumers, spike inflation, and harm American small businesses. If they disagree, now is their time to prove it. My bill will force a nonpartisan study on this Administration's tariffs and how they will impact everyday Americans. Here's what Maryland leaders from the farming industry on the Eastern Shore, to the Chamber of Commerce had to say:

"The dairy industry has worked hard to grow our markets and Trump's tariffs could erase years of hard work and cause a drop in farmers' incomes. Unnecessary and arbitrary tariffs against our best customers puts farmer's livelihoods at risk and the consequences are unpredictable. A study should be undertaken before tariffs are implemented and the predictable retaliation that will undoubtedly follow in response. That's why the Senate must immediately pass Senator Alsobrooks' legislation, the Tariff Transparency Act, so we know exactly how these arbitrary tariffs will impact our livelihoods," **said Judy Gifford, owner and operator, St. Brigid's Farm**.

"Senator Alsobrooks' Tariff Transparency Act is a necessary piece of legislation that will provide the American people with concrete data about the impacts of these tariffs - on consumers, on the small business sector, and on companies' ability to make strategic decisions about hiring and employment," **Benjamin Orr, President, Founder & CEO, Maryland Center on Economic Policy.**

"Small businesses are the backbone of our economy, and I am fearful that these chaotic and non-strategic tariffs from President Trump are going to negatively impact our small businesses and the everyday Americans who rely on us for goods and services. That's why I'm grateful that Senator Alsobrooks has put forward this timely legislation, the Tariff Transparency Act. President Trump has said his tariffs will only cause 'a little disturbance.' This bill will force him to prove it," **said Herman Taylor, President, Minority Business Economic Council, Inc. (MBEC)**.

"The question on everyone's mind is how will these tariffs impact everyday people. Senator Alsobrooks' Tariff Transparency Act will help to answer that question. As the new administration continues to create uncertainty for business owners, having a study that measures how these tariffs will impact the overall economy is an absolute necessity. This is much-needed and timely legislation by Senator Alsobrooks," **said Dewan Clayborn, President & CEO, Central Maryland Chamber of Commerce**.

I will continue to demand transparency from this administration because it's easy to make claims on the news. I would hope the President and my colleagues on the other side of the aisle are willing and ready to be transparent with Marylanders and the American people.

You can find the full text of the bill [here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=14245.6207299.5167025.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.alsobrooks.senate.gov%2fwp-content%2fuploads%2f2025%2f03%2fTariff-Transparency-Act-of-2025-PDF.pdf&redir_log=359490689616657), and read a recent Washington Post article on the bill [here](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=14245.6207299.5167025.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.washingtonpost.com%2fdc-md-va%2f2025%2f03%2f12%2falsobrooks-senate-bill-tariffs%2f&redir_log=286092360536553).

**Howard County Town Hall**

I would like to thank the approximately 1,100 concerned Marylanders who came out to our town hall (and the over 1,000 who joined online) hosted by Senator Chris Van Hollen, Congresswoman Sarah Elfreth, County Executive Calvin Ball and I on Tuesday night. From the unelected Elon Musk and his DOGE operation to those outraged about veterans who have been fired and the uncertainty of critical government programs, we heard your concerns loud and clear. And we are going to continue fighting for all of you.

I said this at the town hall, but I think it bears repeating. The trick of the oppressor is to make sure people feel demoralized, isolated, without hope. But we will not be without hope. We are going to continue fighting, because the arc of the moral universe is long, but it does bend toward justice.

**60th Anniversary of Bloody Sunday**

I was honored to join more than 40 of my colleagues from both sides of the aisle for The Faith & Politics Institute 2025 Congressional Civil Rights Pilgrimage through Montgomery, Selma, and Birmingham, Alabama. Friday, March 7, was the 60th anniversary of Bloody Sunday - the day hundreds of peaceful protesters were attacked with batons wrapped with barbed wire, dogs, and tear gas as they attempted to walk from Selma to Montgomery in support of their right to vote.

I listened to the stories of the foot soldiers, many of whom were never written about in history books, and remain in Alabama until this day. The sacrifices made by so many, including the late Congressman John Lewis, were so significant and remind us that when we need or want change we have to stand up and speak out. Coretta Scott King was instructive to each of us when she said that freedom is never really won. You have to earn it and win it in every generation. I'm ready to win it back and preserve our democracy, and I hope you're ready too.
I know these are difficult times, but please know that I will continue fighting for you every step of the way. We will not give up. We will not give in to this administration, and I ask that you continue to fight alongside us. Please continue to reach out to us. Tell us your story. You can call the office or connect with us at [alsobrooks.senate.gov](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=14245.6207299.5167025.7190525&report_id=&redirect=http%3a%2f%2falsobrooks.senate.gov&redir_log=983830187668200). You can also follow us on [Bluesky](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenAlsobrooks&crop=14245.6207299.5167025.7190525&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fweb-cdn.bsky.app%2fprofile%2falsobrooks.senate.gov&redir_log=255136950764383), Instagram, Facebook, Threads, YouTube and X. Sincerely, Angela Alsobrooks
United States Senator
[Click here to Unsubscribe.](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/EForm.aspx?__cid=SenAlsobrooks&__fid=100022&__crop=14245.6207299.5167025.7190525)

I'm ready to win it back and preserve our democracy, and I hope you're ready too. I know these are difficult times, but please know that I will continue fighting for you every step of the way. We will not give up. We will not give in to this administration, and I ask that you continue to fight alongside us. Please continue to reach out to us. Tell us your story. You can call the office or connect with us at . You can also follow us on , Instagram, Facebook, Threads, YouTube and X. Sincerely, Angela Alsobrooks United States Senator

name: Chairman Eric Underwood
subject: ☕Coffee w/ the Chairman
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NEBRASKA REPUBLICAN PARTY]( "PAID FOR BY THE NEBRASKA REPUBLICAN PARTY")
body:
#50 and a message of stability and clarity. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/4f367447ff42/n41kepo7ku-12766005?e=25844e23a8)
---
| | [ ](https://gop.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=d69c2d2e26&e=25844e23a8)
---
| |
#### Good Morning Republicans,

__ Compassionate, also referenced as merciful.

A core attribute and vital characteristic for believers...show kindness, mercy, and sympathy towards others. Compassion is closely linked to forgiveness and GRACE as believers are encouraged to forgive others as God has forgiven them.

Prioritize values over party politics...we should and CAN examine our own actions first, and then policy and yes, even candidates through a biblical lens. Similarly to eternal salvation, being equipped requires knowledge, understanding, and dedication to education. We must speak and seek, I'd say even hunger for... the truth! Speaking purposely with a purpose or having civil discourse with respect and actively listening to others. Above all, pray and have integrity to be beyond reproach.
_"Remember, it is sin to know what you ought to do and then not do it." James 4:17_
* * *
Coffee with the Chairman #50. __**“I’ve gotta DO something!”**__ This is the message of my very _first_ Coffee newsletter.

With what is happening in our State, with what is happening in our communities...you’ve gotta do something. You have to say; “enough is enough”. I believe that positive change starts with active participation. Don't just sit around and complain about the way things are. Join the hundreds of NEGOP leaders, who have said “enough is enough”. ENGAGE others in dialogue and discussion that increases your knowledge and awareness of the Republican party’s governing apparatus. Get involved and make your voice heard. There are numerous ways to make a tangible impact:
* Join your local county party or political group. This is a great way to connect with like-minded individuals and get involved in local politics.
* Attend county, school board, and city council meetings. By attending these meetings, you can stay informed about the issues that affect your community and have your say on important decisions.
* Speak up and share your views. Your voice matters! Share your thoughts and opinions with your elected officials and fellow citizens.
* Become a county delegate or an elected member of your county or state party. This is an excellent opportunity to shape the direction of the Republican Party in Nebraska.
* Run for a local office, like your school board or city council. If you're passionate about your community, consider running for a local office.

Nebraskans are fed up with the current political climate. In politics, as with any relationship, you can create major influence on people and policy through FEAR. This control removes the choice from voters especially during the Primary Election. It also suppresses quality candidates from emerging who might actually win. Coupled with an election process that does not match the mandate from President Trump focusing on Election Accountability and Voter Verification, I believe(d) a change in culture is due. Republicans of Nebraska...you were and are on the leading edge of change within this country. Change within the Republican party. However, all the change needed for Nebraska will not happen overnight and it will not come to fruition if you waiver. **You have to persist.** You have to see a vision of what this state and country could be again. We took back the party FOR the people, so the people can take back their LOCAL communities, FIRST. I want America First and I want Nebraska First policies.

However, let's also be mindful of say....North Platte First, and Kearney First, and Norfolk First, and Columbus First, etc. Each community is important. You have to engage your local county parties and bring your time, talents, and treasures. You have to engage now because there are even those within our own party who are just waiting for you to lose your activism. Waiting for you to lose your enthusiasm and go home to be good Republicans again who just vote when told and donate when told. The leadership can provide you with the resources and support you need to make a difference. Training, mentorship, and networking opportunities are available to help you get involved and make an impact. Nebraska needs your help to achieve OUR goals for 2025-26. We are focused on:
* Expanding our volunteer base: We need passionate individuals to help us with everything from phone banking to door knocking. 70,000 more Republican voters IS achievable.
* Recruiting and developing candidates: We need qualified and dedicated candidates to run for office at all levels. In collaboration with our county parties, state district caucuses, and national partners, our primary focus is to identify and recruit exceptional **Constitutional & Platform Republican (CPR’s)** candidates. Interviewed and prepared CPR’s will revive the party specifically targeting the local level such as School Boards, ESU’s, Community College Boards, City Councils, NRD’s, Power Boards, and County Department positions.
* Engaging with diverse communities across the state: We believe that the Republican Party should be a big tent party that welcomes people from all walks of life. **Our first goal is a Latino Coalition spearheaded by our National Committeeman William Feely and our Political Director Joe Hagerty.**

We need your help to build a stronger Republican Party in Nebraska. Join us today and make a difference. _**A New Way to Campaign**_ I believe that the power should be in the hands of the voters, not in the hands of consultants and special interests. The focus should be on grassroots organizing, community engagement, and strategic vision implementation. We must be committed to running positive and inclusive campaigns that focus on the issues that matter most to Nebraskans.

I believe that we can win elections at the local level by earning the respect of voters, not by fear-mongering. I believe that if we prioritize honest dialogue, respectful debate, and solutions-oriented policy proposals coupled with relationships to coalition partners like Turning Point Action, Tea Party Patriots, Scott Presler, Cliff Maloney we can be the leading conservative state. These awesome national partners showed a new way to campaign. Relational campaigns built on long term...relationships!

But it takes a lot of time...and effort...and endurance. We must be willing to sacrifice in the short term, to ensure the well being of our state and country in the long term. The people of the party have asked the leaders of the NEGOP to be a voice for the values of the party and no elected official is above the people. The Republican party has suffered from a misalignment between **winning elections** AND **governing solutions through values** , with some leaders holding _**completely**_ different objectives from its voters and value-driven base. I see this misalignment in many other states and has been seemingly widening for decades. You cannot say you are "for" the party if you don't communicate WITH the party ABOUT our principles, no matter WHO is in leadership...or at the very least have Coffee w/ the Chairman to discuss values. I encourage you to contact your elected representatives and ask them to network with the county party and the State Party. I encourage you to highlight the importance of working as a team.

I encourage you to stress the importance of having a State Party that is a voice for the people and values.

I encourage you to take time to read the [Platform Values](https://gop.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=b20813e030&e=25844e23a8), engage in discussions with your elected representatives and ask them where and how we can work together to Defend the Platform.

Let's use the Platform as a shared objective document with which we can have a common truth. This country, this state, your community needs you...everything you concern yourself with on social media or at the dining room table will only find resolution if you, **yes YOU** , get to work now.

Take the first step, it's the hardest step, I promise, and ask the genuine questions... "How can I help?" "What do I need to become a school board member?" "Where can I support the people of the party?"
The truth shall set us free.
Blessings,
---
|
---
| –Chairman Underwood
---
| [Donate](https://gop.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=8d12363564&e=25844e23a8 "Donate")
---
|
---
|

|

|

| [ ](https://gop.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=485c6cc112&e=25844e23a8)
---
|
> Hello Patriots!
>
> Turning Point Action is so excited to share an amazing opportunity with each of you! We wanted to invite you to travel to Wisconsin and chase ballots for Brad Schimel in the WI Supreme Court Race on April 1st.
>
> **Winning the Supreme Court race is crucial to maintaining a majority in Congress.**
>
> Here’s what you need to know:
> * This is a paid opportunity to help take back Wisconsin and chase ballots beginning March 17th through April 1st.
> * Hotel accommodations will be provided and are available from March 17th through April 2nd.
> * In order to be eligible for compensation, you must work a minimum of 5 days.
>
You will be compensated an additional $250 per day for each additional day you work beyond 5.
> * If you are unable to make this commitment, you are welcome to still get a free hotel and come on your own through our general Commit 100 program at [TPAction.com/100](https://gop.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=2b2cbc355a&e=25844e23a8).
>

> * * *
>
> TPAction will be hosting daily training sessions at our WI HQ. More information to come.
> * You will be required to fill out a simple daily report of your progress. This information will be provided to you in further detail when you arrive in Wisconsin.
> * This opportunity is first come first serve. If you are interested in joining us, you must complete the form ASAP.
> * Apply ASAP at [TPAction.com/PAID100](https://gop.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=20505f8ffb&e=25844e23a8) – spots are limited! First come, first serve!
>
Please email commit100@tpaction.com with any questions. | | | | _{"County, Candidate, & Coalition Spotlight" is an informational component of this newsletter ONLY in an effort to expand Republican relationships to our membership. "CC&CS" is not a paid advertisement. "CC&CS" is not an indication of financial support to any officers, governing bodies, or entities of the NEGOP. Any counties or PAC endorsement of candidates at any time are NOT subsequent endorsements by the governing bodies of the NEGOP. The order of content is based on first come and attempted to maintain chronological order to the upcoming week's events.}_
> ---
|
## Weather Update - Lincoln
---
| [ ](https://gop.us21.list-manage.com/track/click?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=8da10ae3ad&e=25844e23a8)
---
|
## Weather Update - Grand Island
---

You can [update your preferences](https://gop.us21.list-manage.com/profile?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=72fdf24cb0&e=25844e23a8&c=e3c73c5ff2) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://gop.us21.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=1d82dcd2e67c4075340c26b37&id=72fdf24cb0&t=b&e=25844e23a8&c=e3c73c5ff2).

"Where can I support the people of the party?" The truth shall set us free. Blessings, | | –Chairman Underwood | "Donate" | | | | | | Hello Patriots! Turning Point Action is so excited to share an amazing opportunity with each of you! We wanted to invite you to travel to Wisconsin and chase ballots for Brad Schimel in the WI Supreme Court Race on April 1st. Winning the Supreme Court race is crucial to maintaining a majority in Congress. Here’s what you need to know: This is a paid opportunity to help take back Wisconsin and chase ballots beginning March 17th through April 1st. Hotel accommodations will be provided and are available from March 17th through April 2nd. In order to be eligible for compensation, you must work a minimum of 5 days. You will be compensated an additional $250 per day for each additional day you work beyond 5. If you are unable to make this commitment, you are welcome to still get a free hotel and come on your own through our general Commit 100 program at . TPAction will be hosting daily training sessions at our WI HQ. More information to come. You will be required to fill out a simple daily report of your progress. This information will be provided to you in further detail when you arrive in Wisconsin. This opportunity is first come first serve. If you are interested in joining us, you must complete the form ASAP. Apply ASAP at – spots are limited! First come, first serve! name: Thomas Carle
subject: Town Hall Event
body:
Hey there!
This is Tom Carle, and I am reaching out today to let you know of an event this Wednesday, March 19, at the Blasdell American Legion from 7 to 10pm.(Erie County) We all need to know that we are still here - and we are working together!

The organizers - Good Neighbors Getting It Done in WNY and Organize for Action in WNY have invited our Congressman to speak, answer our questions and address our concerns. One way or another, we are having ourselves a Town Hall!

All are welcome to attend, speak, listen and ask questions!

I know that many of you are from across the district - I hope to be able to report to you that we have other similar events in the not too distant future that are closer to you - I know that there are wonderful people - just like you - that are working on it!

If the organizers are able to zoom or stream the event, I will post the link on Wednesday!

Thank you for your support in the past and on these types of local events, as we go forward!
Tom

name: Elizabeth Nader
subject: Paterson & Palestine
body:
Peter, Madness, friend. It’s absolute madness. I can hardly believe I’m writing this. In a speech last week that was – to p
|
---
| Peter, Madness, friend. It’s absolute madness. I can hardly believe I’m writing this. In a speech last week that was – to put it mildly – blatantly anti-America, Paterson Mayor André Sayegh called the city the ‘capitol of Palestine’ and the “fourth holiest city in the world.”
---
|
---
| An American city. Calling it the capitol of a country being run and consumed by terrorists under the banner of Hamas. Terrorists that want Israel wiped off the map and the United States with it. Unreal. How do LUNATICS like Mayor Sayegh get elected in the first place? It’s when Common Sense New Jerseyans stay home. When we don’t deem certain elections worth our time. When we don’t do the research. And that starts, by the way, in primaries where we have the chance to vet the candidates who will represent our respective parties. We have to turn this around and that’s why our Club launched the One Less Lemon project, which I’ve been telling you about constantly because _time is running out and this is ESSENTIAL to saving our state._ We need you to give to this project. Soon. It’s that simple.

| Hello, and thank you for taking the time to read this email. This message is part of an ongoing conversation between us, and I want to take a moment to ensure you have full transparency about why you received this message, how your information is handled, and what to expect from future emails. Why You’re Receiving This Email You’re receiving this email because at some point, you opted in to receive updates, news, or information on a specific topic we’ve previously discussed or shared. Whether it was through a subscription, a form submission, or another form of communication, your information was shared willingly, and we respect your decision to connect with us. If you’re wondering about the nature of our correspondence, rest assured that we aim to keep all emails relevant, timely, and free from unnecessary clutter. This includes respecting your inbox and refraining from sending irrelevant messages. Your Privacy is Important We value your trust and take your privacy very seriously. The information you provide is securely stored and never shared, sold, or used outside the purpose for which you provided it. If you ever want to review how we handle your data, feel free to contact us directly. Transparency is our priority, and we’re happy to address any questions you might have. You are always in control of the information you share. If you feel that any part of your subscription or interaction needs clarification, let us know. We’re here to provide accurate answers and ensure your satisfaction. How to Unsubscribe or Manage Your Preferences We understand that everyone’s inbox is different. If you ever find our emails no longer relevant, there’s no hard feelings. You can easily manage your email preferences or unsubscribe using the link provided below. By clicking the unsubscribe link, you’ll be taken to a page where you can either adjust your communication preferences (such as receiving fewer emails or only on specific topics) or completely remove yourself from our list. The process is straightforward, and any changes will take effect promptly. We don’t use tricks or gimmicks to keep you subscribed. Our priority is to ensure that our emails add value to your day, and if they don’t, we respect your decision to part ways. Accessibility and Communication If you have any trouble accessing the unsubscribe page, managing preferences, or understanding why you’re receiving this email, you can reach out directly to our support team at info@commonsense.org. We aim to provide a response within a reasonable time frame and address your concerns effectively. Our communication is designed to be as inclusive as possible, but we know there’s always room for improvement. If you have any feedback on how we can make our emails more accessible or relevant, please don’t hesitate to share. A Commitment to Non-Intrusive Emails We aim to create a non-intrusive communication experience. This means we won’t overwhelm your inbox with excessive messages, and we work hard to ensure our content remains clear and concise. The purpose of this email is to stay connected with you and provide updates or information that we believe is meaningful. If we ever fail to meet these standards, we encourage you to let us know. Feedback, whether positive or constructive, is always appreciated. Your thoughts help us understand how we can do better and improve our communication approach. While we cannot guarantee every suggestion will be implemented, we will take the time to carefully review and consider your input. Contact Information If you’d like to get in touch with us outside of managing your preferences, here’s how you can reach us: Email: info@commonsense.org
Phone: (866) 916-3221 (please note this is a text-only line)
Mailing Address:
Common Sense Club
165 Route 35
Red Bank, New Jersey, 07701 We strive to ensure all communication channels are open and readily available to you. Whether it’s a question, comment, or concern, our team is ready to assist. Legal Information We comply with all applicable laws and regulations regarding email communication. This includes adhering to anti-spam laws and maintaining the highest standards for consent-based communication. Your trust matters, and we work diligently to ensure every email you receive meets these requirements. If you need further clarification on our compliance policies or legal obligations, please feel free to ask. Transparency and accountability are central to our communication strategy, and we’re happy to provide additional details if needed. A Final Note Emails are one of the many ways we stay connected, but we understand they aren’t perfect for everyone. If there’s another way you’d prefer to communicate or stay updated, let us know. Whether it’s through social media, a direct call, or another channel, we’re open to finding the most effective way to share information with you. We want to emphasize that our goal is never to disrupt or clutter your inbox. Every email sent is intended to provide value, and we genuinely appreciate your time and attention. Thank you for allowing us to stay in touch with you. This footer was designed to ensure transparency, provide essential information, and give you full control over your communication preferences. We hope it meets your expectations, but if there’s anything you’d like to see improved, we’re always here to listen.
---

I can hardly believe I’m writing this. In a speech last week that was – to put it mildly – blatantly anti-America, Paterson Mayor André Sayegh called the city the ‘capitol of Palestine’ and the “fourth holiest city in the world.” | | An American city. Calling it the capitol of a country being run and consumed by terrorists under the banner of Hamas. Terrorists that want Israel wiped off the map and the United States with it. Unreal. How do LUNATICS like Mayor Sayegh get elected in the first place? It’s when Common Sense New Jerseyans stay home. When we don’t deem certain elections worth our time. When we don’t do the research. And that starts, by the way, in primaries where we have the chance to vet the candidates who will represent our respective parties. We have to turn this around and that’s why our Club launched the One Less Lemon project, which I’ve been telling you about constantly because time is running out and this is ESSENTIAL to saving our state. We need you to give to this project. Soon. It’s that simple. | | == | | == | | == | | == | We have a plan to turn out more common sense voters across the state to vote, to vote in primaries, and to vote by mail (because we need to beat the Left at their own game). Peter,

Medicaid covers **42% of births** in the U.S., providing essential prenatal, postpartum, and reproductive care. Now, Congress is considering slashing **$880 billion** from Medicaid—a **devastating attack on maternal health and reproductive rights.** | [**SAY NO**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/104049008/534120930/1668202114?sourceid=1150540&utm_campaign=25emeus-0303aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=medicaid&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2Y2E1YzJlMi1mOTAzLWYwMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PZgWbvqkpU3ouY2aB9hlknj3qmQdyYvm8H6EVfsyz18=&emci=106ea956-6c03-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=6ca5c2e2-f903-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=954237 "SAY NO")
---
[**This proposal would have catastrophic consequences, especially for Black, Indigenous, rural, and low-income communities—those most likely to die from birth-related complications due to systemic neglect and lack of access to care. Expanding Medicaid coverage has saved lives—cutting it will do the opposite.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/104049010/534120933/1668202114?sourceid=1150540&utm_campaign=25emeus-0303aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=medicaid&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2Y2E1YzJlMi1mOTAzLWYwMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PZgWbvqkpU3ouY2aB9hlknj3qmQdyYvm8H6EVfsyz18=&emci=106ea956-6c03-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=6ca5c2e2-f903-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=954237)

We cannot afford to go backward. Congress must reject these dangerous cuts and protect the health and rights of millions.

[**Add your name now: Tell Congress to keep their #HandsOffMedicaid.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/104049012/534120935/1668202114?sourceid=1150540&utm_campaign=25emeus-0303aax-on&utm_medium=ea&utm_source=email&utm_content=medicaid&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9DRlJSL0NGUlIvMS85NTk4MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2Y2E1YzJlMi1mOTAzLWYwMTEtOTBjZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PZgWbvqkpU3ouY2aB9hlknj3qmQdyYvm8H6EVfsyz18=&emci=106ea956-6c03-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&emdi=6ca5c2e2-f903-f011-90cd-0022482a9fb7&ceid=954237)
