name: Gloria Johnson
subject: Happening in Tennessee
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/80619/images/Screenshot%202025-03-17%20095807.png]
Photo: TIRRC [https://www.instagram.com/tnimmigrant/]
Peter,
If you’re reading this, then let me start by saying: THANK YOU. Thank you for your support and for standing tall - not just for me and last year’s US Senate campaign, but for ALL Tennesseans. While we clearly have more work to do to turn things around in Tennessee, I can’t tell you how encouraged I am by the outpouring of support I’ve witnessed firsthand from good, decent people all across this state – fighting for a better Tennessee, just like you.
But now Trump is President and we face what seems like a fire hose of horrible policies coming at us from both the national and local level. I keep getting asked the same questions: What can we do? Where do we start? While I don’t pretend to have all the answers, I’m writing to you today because I know for sure that the time for us to take action is NOW . And I want to share with you my #1 suggestion for how and where to do just that. But first, a little background...
I’m now back at the state legislature, proudly representing my Knoxville community. And as you’ve probably seen by now, Tennessee’s MAGA supermajority is back to churning out harmful, hateful legislation that’s intended to harm anyone who doesn’t look, think, or vote like them. They don’t even pretend to have any ideas to make life a little easier, safer, or more affordable for Tennesseans - just more extreme ideological grandstanding, with vulnerable children as collateral.
While MAGA Republicans have any number of bad bills we’d like to defeat, there’s one particularly disgraceful bill that I want to tell you about. Just last week, House Bill 793 passed an important committee [https://www.tennessean.com/story/news/politics/2025/03/11/tennessee-immigration-student-bill-advances-in-house-protests-mount/82267835007/?tbref=hp] ; it’s similar to a bill that also recently passed a Senate committee, which means it’s likely headed to a full floor vote soon. In short, this bill is designed to deny K-12 public education to children based on their immigration status . That idea defies logic, basic human decency, and long standing practice and constitutional protections of equal access to education.
With Trump’s hand-picked Supreme Court, MAGA Republicans are itching to overturn another basic human right, just like they did with Roe v. Wade... and this time their targets are children. It’s nothing short of despicable. Regardless of how anyone feels about our immigration system and how best to improve it, we should all agree that every child deserves an education and that denying them an education is harmful to ALL of us and the America we want for our future.
If you believe as I do, that education is for ALL children, no matter where they’re from , then this moment right now is when you are needed most by saying you’re IN and willing to take action that will actually make a difference with those fighting back: [https://www.educationforalltn.org/petition?fn=Peter&ln=Peter&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com&mp=&contactdata=AQjZfEmeJSkqaUQzyhT0/+OV4Rq3s5OJH/3Z9YM+dlPyo7T6A3RB0ldZI2ovjrua19zAKSmc2u3R6fVX7EvKDvgg20CgR8+WxFMUw06u3iLxWuU2hmFmtprrfSADapB0UqmkAhnnY7HZu/qQhWj62pDIKytYdocqBdRzSnSJMkQ=&ms=GloriaJohnson]
Add Your Name [https://www.educationforalltn.org/petition?fn=Peter&ln=Peter&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com&mp=&contactdata=AQjZfEmeJSkqaUQzyhT0/+OV4Rq3s5OJH/3Z9YM+dlPyo7T6A3RB0ldZI2ovjrua19zAKSmc2u3R6fVX7EvKDvgg20CgR8+WxFMUw06u3iLxWuU2hmFmtprrfSADapB0UqmkAhnnY7HZu/qQhWj62pDIKytYdocqBdRzSnSJMkQ=&ms=GloriaJohnson]
You will immediately be followed up with all the ways you can make a difference: contacting likely supportive constituents of targeted lawmakers voting on this bill, important stories to share online, virtual and in-person volunteer opportunities as early as today or tomorrow, and more.
I will keep you posted as this and other harmful legislation takes shape and ways you can take action to help. In the words of Rev. Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., “You don’t have to see the whole staircase, just take the first step .” Thank you so much for standing with me and for taking this important step on behalf of our kids.
With you,
Gloria
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/80619/images/Screenshot%202025-03-17%20101734.png]
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
Click here if you’d like to only receive our most important email updates. [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Sh6Bw1VRNEmwDbBMi1-k5w2]
Click here to unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/IR4yzv3oX0-QrAUELqEcpA2?unsubscribedata=WKWE8IpmVFNDyCHWdr52KFp%2BiYHKwz5yhm7c6STurq5RKiOLRJczfPug4fzaElBO%2Bft9Wx8TlLHP0mKSAl3lwycojRu2EqTWmqY8%2FQd6IHKNIqh%2Be5M7x9BFEPrdg13AqF%2BekpKSXGXKM1SKl6c96bOmR%2FfazAVMFK53vMIFbXzE6uav%2FH%2BK7rkDEcKw4EzC%2Fgaq8AIKrjfiBX4LmTIdYvct6rf%2Fb3LmtsPGvoHK7HR3P9P08jIS58oBCFe%2FHYFlXv%2FKHoBESWCB7v1o8NToZwh449atDL%2FsbBMgs0%2BOsBOt3zxOegmXzUMpUIlDxh8hLnisdgl9VQmiY2e8xgqPTL39vlJlr1wWunOeKsUVKptao1Mb8lX2bGQ95InlR99MGO%2FzS3T7WOmawmt5rWsRv2%2BXb%2Fjrb9BXoYz1ak8r4Rq9Y%2BBjBf34IW4KVHjm3FVa .

* * *
* **TOP NEWS**

"**Measles outbreak grows to 321 cases across three states** ," Dallas Morning News' **Emily Brindley** -- "The measles outbreak that began in West Texas has now grown to [more than 300 cases](https://substack.com/redirect/42f0082e-c433-43a8-a8ee-146f391141d7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) across three states.

The Texas Department of State Health Services reported Tuesday morning that Texas [now has 279 confirmed measles cases](https://substack.com/redirect/50ce0519-24b0-4d53-89d8-a21a1871eeaf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the outbreak. That number does not include at least four additional measles cases that have been reported in Texas, but which are not not connected to the outbreak.

Texas' case count also does not include cases reported in neighboring New Mexico and Oklahoma.

The New Mexico Department of Health on Friday [reported 38 measles cases](https://substack.com/redirect/69be7d5d-6fa6-4e34-9eea-af55f3b4de74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in two counties. The vast majority of those cases are in New Mexico's Lea County, which directly borders Texas' Gaines County, where the outbreak originated.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/3602771d-9321-480b-9125-1fb9e9f4866d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The Oklahoma State Department of Health has reported a total of [four probable measles cases](https://substack.com/redirect/3bc5b035-ec7b-497e-ae5f-b7d9f97f0eb5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which are all believed to be connected to the Texas and New Mexico outbreak.

In Tuesday's update, the Texas Department of State Health Services did not report any new counties with cases.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/3602771d-9321-480b-9125-1fb9e9f4866d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Last week, the state [reported five measles cases in northeast Texas](https://substack.com/redirect/0da429c9-9b7c-443f-bf4a-278db957a45d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Those cases, in Lamar County, were the first instance of the outbreak spreading outside of West Texas and the Panhandle.

Since the outbreak began, a total of 36 people in Texas have been hospitalized. In Texas, one child, who was not vaccinated, [has died](https://substack.com/redirect/51666b49-cb4a-4801-96af-7529c4017d10?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). New Mexico has also reported [one adult death in connection](https://substack.com/redirect/2af395ef-5c38-4da8-be0c-ef160ba91451?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with the outbreak.

Measles is a highly contagious virus that spreads primarily among unvaccinated people. The disease has been considered eliminated in the U.S. since 2000, but there have been outbreaks across the country in communities with low vaccination rates.

Of the 279 measles cases in Texas, only two were identified in people who have been vaccinated. (State officials previously reported five cases among vaccinated people, but revised the number after learning that two cases were people vaccinated after exposure to the virus, and a third case was a measles vaccine reaction as opposed to a measles infection.)

The remaining 277 cases in Texas were among people who were either unvaccinated or whose vaccination status is unknown, according to the state.

The two-dose measles-mumps-rubella vaccine is highly effective at preventing measles infection. It's recommended for children beginning at about 12 months old and for all adults, with the exception of people who are pregnant or severely immunocompromised.

Local health authorities across Texas are offering the vaccine at clinics. People can also contact their doctor or pharmacy to ask about the vaccine." [DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/752a2d7b-0da4-4711-85b2-ca6001e035c7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

TOP NEWS "Measles outbreak grows to 321 cases across three states ," Dallas Morning News' Emily Brindley -- "The measles outbreak that began in West Texas has now grown to across three states. The Texas Department of State Health Services reported Tuesday morning that Texas from the outbreak. That number does not include at least four additional measles cases that have been reported in Texas, but which are not not connected to the outbreak. Texas' case count also does not include cases reported in neighboring New Mexico and Oklahoma. The New Mexico Department of Health on Friday in two counties. The vast majority of those cases are in New Mexico's Lea County, which directly borders Texas' Gaines County, where the outbreak originated. The Oklahoma State Department of Health has reported a total of , which are all believed to be connected to the Texas and New Mexico outbreak. In Tuesday's update, the Texas Department of State Health Services did not report any new counties with cases. Last week, the state . Those cases, in Lamar County, were the first instance of the outbreak spreading outside of West Texas and the Panhandle. Since the outbreak began, a total of 36 people in Texas have been hospitalized. In Texas, one child, who was not vaccinated, . New Mexico has also reported with the outbreak. Measles is a highly contagious virus that spreads primarily among unvaccinated people. The disease has been considered eliminated in the U.S. since 2000, but there have been outbreaks across the country in communities with low vaccination rates. Of the 279 measles cases in Texas, only two were identified in people who have been vaccinated. (State officials previously reported five cases among vaccinated people, but revised the number after learning that two cases were people vaccinated after exposure to the virus, and a third case was a measles vaccine reaction as opposed to a measles infection.) The remaining 277 cases in Texas were among people who were either unvaccinated or whose vaccination status is unknown, according to the state. The two-dose measles-mumps-rubella vaccine is highly effective at preventing measles infection. It's recommended for children beginning at about 12 months old and for all adults, with the exception of people who are pregnant or severely immunocompromised. Local health authorities across Texas are offering the vaccine at clinics. People can also contact their doctor or pharmacy to ask about the vaccine." name: Kimberly Bretches
subject: The George Floyd riots continue to haunt my husband
body:
| [View in Web Browser](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJw8kM1u6jAQhZ_G2RHFYydOFl7cXOSqjdRKUBZ0ZyY2mMYkcf7E21dQynbmmzmfjvZd4-xVElESgJOrBwJA2D8C0O8XH6azr15W2dvHedVn7z69beH_A53hCV9QV9X31m_EZzmUWf96KUI_0yc-XjvzhB-hsfHaNbepWEe1ZLZOeGQkFRwYp4XIopMsBM0TCyI1KJCaGlieGY4pItaWFofISUggTRjNgaXAeGyR0STX1qA5INeW8OSeE2tvgkNNh3HRIbStj7H1USNP49gNdzFFQC2hjS8TNmYafs-w9QTUQ5mAmgmoiZXVTm22R0aYupfG1jT72u_Wur9GQdbd4prGDZkgPDn-vYlmCT8BAAD__9tib5g)
| | The same city that gave Chance this award is trying to put him in prison for life for doing his job.
---
| |
---
Friend, This is a photo of me and my husband, **Officer Chance Bretches** , on the day Chance received the Lifesaving Medal With Valor for rescuing a driver from a crashed car that had caught on fire.
---
[](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJw8j8GOmzAQhp_G3ED22GA4cFiWELVJWym72qon5Njjjbc4EAOJ8vYrIpSLJX_zaf75lR86Z-8lkRUBODkzEgDCXwjA5d_Nh_nL77Zx9vPPV3zJfvt0mcLrql7hKZ-12u3-v_mDfK_GKrv8OBfhcmVPfboP-JTX0AS9ct1CZR2ZkltDRYQlkwK4YIXMolOZokWLaSoklcrmRrIUivQocsV5JqSNXAkUUspZDjwFLhKrOaO5sqjxqIWyRNBHTqI8BqcVG6ebCqHvfaJ7H3XlaZqG8XFYQ6AZUc8BE3VG00-LQaDpsDOWQGMKKhVDFmtjIRbsqOP8mLIYaMFznklqBC5beDNPvh37OWgkvK7qbXzYfBDIFuzRuNkTXm9-rWTCsPxvga1AKz8o93kmvF7Lybaqt-1-s6-b9rX6ux9P7WHz0b69vxzi5YlCaYab6zo3ZpII-vlovPS7lvAdAAD__2JBkP0)
---
This photo used to be one of my favorites. I was so proud of my husband that day. Now looking at it makes me cry. Because the same city that gave Chance this award is trying to put him in prison **for life** for doing his job. **[That’s why I’m humbly asking you to help me save my husband.](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJw8j8GOmzAQhp_G3ED22GA4cFiWELVJWym72qon5Njjjbc4EAOJ8vYrIpSLJX_zaf75lR86Z-8lkRUBODkzEgDCXwjA5d_Nh_nL77Zx9vPPV3zJfvt0mcLrql7hKZ-12u3-v_mDfK_GKrv8OBfhcmVPfboP-JTX0AS9ct1CZR2ZkltDRYQlkwK4YIXMolOZokWLaSoklcrmRrIUivQocsV5JqSNXAkUUspZDjwFLhKrOaO5sqjxqIWyRNBHTqI8BqcVG6ebCqHvfaJ7H3XlaZqG8XFYQ6AZUc8BE3VG00-LQaDpsDOWQGMKKhVDFmtjIRbsqOP8mLIYaMFznklqBC5beDNPvh37OWgkvK7qbXzYfBDIFuzRuNkTXm9-rWTCsPxvga1AKz8o93kmvF7Lybaqt-1-s6-b9rX6ux9P7WHz0b69vxzi5YlCaYab6zo3ZpII-vlovPS7lvAdAAD__2JBkP0)****** Friend, it wasn’t that long ago that Chance was a decorated police officer with the Austin, Texas Police Department. When my husband and his partner were assigned to the Street Narcotics Unit, I was terrified. After all, they’re the ones who deal with violent drug cartels. But Chance was brave. He was willing to put his life in danger to keep our city safe. Little did we know the biggest threat Chance would face wasn’t a dangerous drug dealer... ...but a radical, Soros-funded prosecutor. You see, Chance was called in to help disperse violent protestors during the violent George Floyd riots in Austin in May 2020. The police department issued Chance a beanbag launcher because they are supposed to be effective without causing serious injury. So, when a rioter hurled rocks at police officers, Chance discharged two bean bags at him. Another protestor stepped in front of the rioter at the exact wrong moment and was struck in the hand. It was a freak accident – but because a cop fired the non-lethal weapon, it instantly becomes a felony to a Soros-backed prosecutor. That radical Soros-funded prosecutor charged my husband with FELONY ASSAULT. **I learned that the prosecutor in this case owes his election to a wad of campaign cash from none other than radical Leftist billionaire George Soros.** That was when I realized that the anti-cop crowd is trying to make an example out of my husband. They’ll do everything in their power to make cops pay. It makes me wonder why anyone would ever sign up to join law enforcement these days. But over 13 years ago, my husband did. Chance put his life on the line to protect his community countless times, and life behind bars could be his “reward.” And on top of that, his trial was postponed for **_a fifth time_** and pushed back to start July 7, 2025. Chance has had this charge hanging over his head for the past FIVE years and the trial will finally be his opportunity for justice. But even if my husband’s good name is cleared, there is no way we could hope to pay the mountain of legal bills. That’s why I’m so thankful for the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund and patriots who back the blue. Jason Johnson and the LELDF board organize and fund the successful legal defense of good cops wrongly charged for crimes – like my husband. LELDF helps hire the best attorneys and secure expert witnesses who will testify to the truth – without being influenced by political paybacks. You’ve read my email this far so I’m going to ask as boldly as I can: **[**Will you help by sending a gift to LELDF to help defend my husband and the other good police officers who have been wrongly accused?**](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJw8j8GOmzAQhp_G3ED22GA4cFiWELVJWym72qon5Njjjbc4EAOJ8vYrIpSLJX_zaf75lR86Z-8lkRUBODkzEgDCXwjA5d_Nh_nL77Zx9vPPV3zJfvt0mcLrql7hKZ-12u3-v_mDfK_GKrv8OBfhcmVPfboP-JTX0AS9ct1CZR2ZkltDRYQlkwK4YIXMolOZokWLaSoklcrmRrIUivQocsV5JqSNXAkUUspZDjwFLhKrOaO5sqjxqIWyRNBHTqI8BqcVG6ebCqHvfaJ7H3XlaZqG8XFYQ6AZUc8BE3VG00-LQaDpsDOWQGMKKhVDFmtjIRbsqOP8mLIYaMFznklqBC5beDNPvh37OWgkvK7qbXzYfBDIFuzRuNkTXm9-rWTCsPxvga1AKz8o93kmvF7Lybaqt-1-s6-b9rX6ux9P7WHz0b69vxzi5YlCaYab6zo3ZpII-vlovPS7lvAdAAD__2JBkP0)** Right now, Chance’s legal team is working day and night to prepare for his trial that starts on July 7, 2025. They know a good man’s life is at stake. So with that in mind, please know that any contribution is tax-deductible. Please make your gift right away if possible. I can never express how grateful I am to you – in advance – for helping my husband.
---

With hope and thanks,
---
Kimberly Bretches
---
---
|
---
_More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visit. And remember –Blue Lives Matter!_ © Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund | [www.PoliceDefense.org](https://email.email.america1stwarroom.com/c/eJw8ks2SqjoAhJ8Gd8ci4W9cuHBGcEAJI8agbG4lgEMgwQioA09_C8-pWXbypbtT1VQqwS_DUnPeNQhLnncahJqx0iC8nZ-yvVdyu_ljB1H152YjaU238OMf-oC_cJPR7bY-yNjB7927ffObRXt7gF-8H1TxC_8LnReScjGdOutZvjQuuW7OiiVwTGiYYOHYs3LJmGkz5wJsw1xkDFDHMB1WLN4sVuSQWfaML6EOLd0Ab9CwoGHOL5kB9Dd6KbKCZSa9aKb-yplTWbQ8o6Drn7Rtr1c5z65yJpZl36vuVczToNe39Vx-z7Nr0xXtg_b8UdCs59emKZ7d9EKDXqZBz9GgVwxBSQ--jcYQhNgdwjHsfHnkEQ8eTJKGJaQJ194qhaCkyfN-hove50-eyZpH3DfCamWg9ZHvPoIhO_i2X62GXeIPaB0O4YeuR5uAn6EPz-PKOo9HPdrEMsLvNZKkRFVZR-ucoySuQu53vtR5JNOSfSKx-wgAffntJ2_AuG_7E9O8ul3zz_iZjdfHzogHOlh9OlgVg9aYngJ4PsUql6Q8G7Fi0Lqnp3zcSfRgh0W1G7NHehJbuvHwAadRUes_mMRecQQ2PqGAEaIwFNtcBj2RFicgveNjvNmT6xAmZUmMHCTYt3JXqByKiiX9xAehDm57jKLYIwq7aFskaPIPaO1Z2MuD4mi5B0y-juMKYBK3ob5wqaEorT07EbEXeuJGpPoiLrFxHR-oC1w6Boh4tZ4kqkKewIepH35XGAJKN4GP5U-4168_Z4ICVItbkoi_WopJqxcv_v6Hwdg_NnlVyAXEa1SHMLbQKedhUz4THCQUqFMifzirrQ5vdKsgyoo2Qh4Pi4SevO5k5Ju8URyJ1GUukqkRN7kXG36ja9Dz1X-JsU53969PqWQR-J-r73Y_a5e5enIheGc7mql_vzY8LfaxhP8HAAD__-7ENL0)

Jason Johnson and the LELDF board organize and fund the successful legal defense of good cops wrongly charged for crimes – like my husband. LELDF helps hire the best attorneys and secure expert witnesses who will testify to the truth – without being influenced by political paybacks. You’ve read my email this far so I’m going to ask as boldly as I can: Right now, Chance’s legal team is working day and night to prepare for his trial that starts on July 7, 2025. They know a good man’s life is at stake. So with that in mind, please know that any contribution is tax-deductible. Please make your gift right away if possible. I can never express how grateful I am to you – in advance – for helping my husband. With hope and thanks, Kimberly Bretches | More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visit. And remember –Blue Lives Matter! © Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund | 2560 Huntington Ave. Suite 203, Alexandria, VA, 22303
